title: Reusable AI Playbooks
summary: Turn successful AI workflows into repeatable playbooks that others can use, review, and improve.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: What A Playbook Actually Is
lessons.subtitle: A playbook is a reusable workflow with context, prompts, review rules, and examples.
lessons.objective: Define playbooks in a way that makes them useful for individuals and teams.
lessons.useWhen: A playbook is a reusable workflow with context, prompts, review rules, and examples.
lessons.whyItMatters: A good playbook captures not only prompt wording but also the surrounding workflow that makes the output trustworthy.

coreConcepts.title: Playbooks Capture Workflow
coreConcepts.text: They include when to use the workflow, what inputs are needed, and what review is required.

coreConcepts.title: Playbooks Reduce Reinvention
coreConcepts.text: They turn trial-and-error into a reusable starting point.

coreConcepts.title: Playbooks Improve Team Consistency
coreConcepts.text: People can produce more consistent work when the structure and review rules are shared.

workedExample.scenario: A team has one person who is unusually effective with AI for customer recap notes.
workedExample.approach: That person turns their working method into a playbook with a context block, prompt, and review checklist.
workedExample.result: Others can start from a proven workflow instead of copying random prompts.
workedExample.verification: The team tests the playbook on a second case before sharing it broadly.

doThisNow.task: Identify one AI workflow you should convert into a playbook.

commonMistakes: Treating a single prompt as a full playbook.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

sourceNotes: Reviewed for business IC relevance and durable workflow guidance.
Keep verification and organizational policy checks explicit in future revisions.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Design The Inputs First
lessons.subtitle: Most playbooks live or die by the quality of their inputs.
lessons.objective: Define the input package needed to make a playbook reliable.
lessons.useWhen: Most playbooks live or die by the quality of their inputs.
lessons.whyItMatters: Playbooks fail when they depend on hidden knowledge or messy context that is never documented.

coreConcepts.title: List Required Inputs
coreConcepts.text: Templates, notes, examples, approved facts, and output destinations should be explicit.

coreConcepts.title: Show What Good Looks Like
coreConcepts.text: An example output or completed case makes the playbook easier to use.

coreConcepts.title: Remove Hidden Assumptions
coreConcepts.text: If the playbook assumes internal jargon or unwritten policy, capture that openly.

workedExample.scenario: A writing playbook works for its creator but fails for teammates.
workedExample.approach: They discover the missing ingredient was the approved tone guide and a sample output.
workedExample.result: The playbook becomes much more reusable once the inputs are explicit.
workedExample.verification: A teammate uses the playbook independently to confirm the input list is complete.

doThisNow.task: Write the required input list for one playbook you want to create.
steps: List the approval or policy rules.

commonMistakes: Assuming others know the hidden context.

roleVariants.manager: Model safe adoption through small pilots, clear examples, and lightweight team habits.
roleVariants.writer: Document the prompts, examples, and edits that help others replicate quality.
roleVariants.analyst: Define simple measures and evidence standards so adoption conversations stay grounded.
roleVariants.operator: Focus on workflow change and training, not just tool access.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Use System Prompts And Guidance Carefully
lessons.subtitle: Persistent instructions are valuable when they encode standards, not wishful thinking.
lessons.objective: Write stable guidance for repeated AI workflows without overcomplicating the setup.
lessons.useWhen: Persistent instructions are valuable when they encode standards, not wishful thinking.
lessons.whyItMatters: Persistent instructions can save time, but only when they encode durable standards and real boundaries.

coreConcepts.title: Encode Standards
coreConcepts.text: Voice, structure, prohibited moves, and review rules are good candidates for persistent guidance.

coreConcepts.title: Do Not Encode Unverified Assumptions
coreConcepts.text: Persistent instructions should not hard-code facts that change often.

coreConcepts.title: Keep It Readable
coreConcepts.text: If nobody can understand or update the guidance, the playbook will decay quickly.

workedExample.scenario: A team uses a persistent instruction set for executive updates.
workedExample.approach: They encode style, format, and review rules, but keep project facts in the task-specific prompt.
workedExample.result: The setup stays reusable without going stale immediately.
workedExample.verification: They review the persistent guidance quarterly to remove outdated assumptions.

doThisNow.task: Split one prompt into durable guidance and task-specific context.
steps: Move changing facts into the task prompt.

commonMistakes: Packing every fact into persistent instructions.
Making guidance too long to maintain.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Test And Improve Playbooks
lessons.subtitle: A playbook is not finished when it works once.
lessons.objective: Create a lightweight test method for AI playbooks.
lessons.useWhen: A playbook is not finished when it works once.
lessons.whyItMatters: Playbooks should survive more than one happy-path example.

coreConcepts.title: Test On Multiple Cases
coreConcepts.text: A playbook should work on varied inputs from the same workflow.

coreConcepts.title: Track Failure Modes
coreConcepts.text: Most playbook improvement comes from seeing where it breaks, not where it shines.

coreConcepts.title: Update The Smallest Useful Thing
coreConcepts.text: Revise the missing input, unclear constraint, or review step before rewriting the whole playbook.

workedExample.scenario: A support recap playbook works well on simple tickets but fails on escalations.
workedExample.approach: The team adds an escalation branch and a new review rule instead of rewriting the full playbook.
workedExample.result: The playbook becomes more reliable while staying simple.
workedExample.verification: They retest it on another escalation case to confirm the revision helped.

doThisNow.task: Test one playbook on a second real case and record what broke.
steps: Run the playbook on a new case.

commonMistakes: Overhauling the playbook without diagnosing the failure.

lessons.sequenceRationale: This lesson exists here to move the learner from understanding the pattern to applying it on a live work artifact.
lessons.title: Maintain A Shared Playbook Library
lessons.subtitle: The value grows when others can find, trust, and update the right workflow.
lessons.objective: Create a simple shared library for team playbooks.
lessons.useWhen: The value grows when others can find, trust, and update the right workflow.
lessons.whyItMatters: A library makes AI knowledge operational. It also reduces repeated errors and duplicated effort.

coreConcepts.title: Make Entries Scannable
coreConcepts.text: Use case, inputs, prompt pattern, review checklist, owner, and last review date should be visible.

coreConcepts.title: Assign Ownership
coreConcepts.text: Someone should be responsible for updating the playbook when the workflow changes.

coreConcepts.title: Prune Aggressively
coreConcepts.text: Outdated or unused playbooks create noise and reduce trust.

workedExample.scenario: A team has dozens of saved prompts spread across chat threads and docs.
workedExample.approach: They move the useful ones into a shared library with ownership and last-reviewed dates.
workedExample.result: The best workflows become easier to discover and maintain.
workedExample.verification: They remove duplicate entries and test the top three playbooks to ensure they still work.

doThisNow.task: Create one clean shared library entry from a playbook you trust.
steps: Write the use case and owner.
Add the prompt pattern and review rules.

outcomes: Turn good prompts into reusable playbooks.
Capture inputs, review rules, and examples that others can follow.
nextModuleBridge: Next, move from personal effectiveness into practical team adoption.
